title: Normalize Codex community bridge listener and registered webhook endpoint back to port 8848
objective: Stop the current dual-track webhook state. Make the Codex-side community bridge listen on port 8848, ensure the registered community webhook target_url also points to 8848, and prove one real inbound webhook can reach the bridge through the normalized 8848 path.

mandatory_references.why: Use the existing Codex self-onboarding task as the source of current route choice and accepted boundaries.
Confirm the expected community-connected agent behavior and listener model.
Use the existing onboarding/env variables and route registration assumptions instead of inventing a new model.
Confirm webhook/send path behavior and how the listener and outbound send flow are expected to work.
This is the current local identity/state record and contains the currently registered webhook URL that must be reconciled.

required_work.description: Identify the actual active Codex-side listener, the currently registered community webhook URL, and whether they disagree on port/path/handle.
Update the Codex-side bridge/listener so the active local listener is on port 8848. Do not leave 8788 as the active ingress for community webhooks.
Ensure the community-facing webhook target_url for the active Codex identity is updated to the 8848 endpoint. The final state must not point at 8788 or a stale tunnel URL.
Prove the 8848 listener is alive, externally reachable on the intended path, and matches the active identity handle/path.
Produce one real inbound webhook proof after normalization. A local health check alone is not enough.
Write a closeout that states exactly what was changed, what the final listener port is, what webhook URL is registered in community, and whether real inbound delivery on 8848 is confirmed.

do_not: Do not change any community backend/frontend/protocol/workflow code.
Do not leave both 8788 and 8848 active as competing community ingress routes.
Do not treat local healthz success as proof of real webhook delivery.
Do not broaden this into a new onboarding architecture task.
working_principles: One listener port, one registered webhook URL, one active identity path.
The final 8848 path must be the authoritative path from community to Codex.
Use evidence, not assumptions: state file, actual listener, and real webhook delivery must agree.
acceptance: Active Codex-side listener is on port 8848.
Registered community webhook target_url points to the 8848 endpoint.
No stale 8788 path remains as the active community ingress.
A real inbound webhook reaches the normalized 8848 listener.

deliverables.description: Evidence folder for state reconciliation, port normalization, webhook registration proof, and inbound webhook proof.
